name: Kit: Cavalier
raw_text:
Cavalier 
Description: The Cavalier Is 
the ultimate mounted warrior of 
cJvillzed c ultures. especially 
those of Middle Ages technology 
and oullook. ln a campaign, he's 
the shining knight who leads his 
fellows on an eternal quest for 
lru Lh. justice, and the elimlna· 
tion of evil. To w e world at large. 
he's a mighty hero. To his 
friends a nd allies. he's a staunch 
friend . a tireless cheerleader. 
and of len an overenthusiastic 
pain in the neck. 
This is a good Warrior Kit for 
paladIns to take. It can be argued that paladins look some· 
thing like this already. but thaI 
Isn't necessarily so: Only pala· 
dins of cultures resembling medieval Europe would look like 
this (a paladin of a Japanesetype culture. a paladin of a 
Polyneslan·like culture, and a 
paladin of a culture resembling 
later Renaissance Europe would 
all be very different from the 
Cavalier). Therefore. a paladin 
who wants to look every inch 
the shining knight should take 
the Cavalier Warrior Kit. 
The Cavalier kit resembles 
the Noble Warrior kit (q.v.) in 
II 
t.hat both are noblemen war· 
riors. but the Noble Warrior is 
primarily mterested In defendIng the rights and maintaining 
the status quo orhls social class. 
while the Cavalier pursues 
loftier goals. 
1b be a cavalier. the character 
must be of any good aUgnment 
(chaotic good, neutral good, lawful good) and have at least the 
fo llowing 
minimum a bfllty 
scores: Strength 15. Dexterity 
15. ConslftutJon 15. Intel1lgence 
10. Wisdom 10. 
Also. the character must be· 
long to the noble social class in 
the campaign. It's up to the OM 
to determine whether this Is 
possible. If his campaign uses a 
random die-roll to determine 
who's nobility and who Isn't, 
then Lhe character must first 
successfully roll to be noble in 
order to bea Cavalier. 'fi t's more 
of a role·playlng exercise in the 
campaign. then any character 
who takes the Cavalier Warrior 
Kit will be presum ed to be of the 
nObi lity. (This doesn 't mean 
that he has a lot of money; It's 
quite likely tbat he belongs toan 
impoveris hed noble family, one 
with a lot of honorable tradition 
bUI no mODey to speak of.) 
Fighters and PaladIns may be 
Cava liers: Rangers may not. 
Only humans. elves. and halfelves may be Cavaliers. 
Role: In his own and similar 
cultures . the Ca valier Is a 
mighty hero who has the reo 
spect of tile majority of the pop. 
ulatlon (the criminal classes and 
evil characters excepted). He 
has the good·wUl of the people. 
(reflected as bonuses to his reaction rol\s). but the people also 
make many demands of him: 
When there's da nger. when 
someone is in trouble, the peo· 
pie turn to the Cavalier for help. 
This cha racter does not gel 
much time for rest and relaxa· 
Uon. 
Secondary SkHls: If you're 
using the Scc.ondary Skills

rules, the Cavalier must lake 
Croom. 
Weapon Proficiencies: ReqUired: Lance (any: player 
choice) and Sword (any: player 
choice). Recommended: All 
other Lances. all other Swords. 
all Horsemen's weapons, Dag· 
ger, Spear. Javelin. 
Nonweapon Proficiencies: 
Bonus Proflclcncles: Riding. 
(Land·based. horse). EUqueue. 
Recommended: Animal Han· 
dllng, Animal Training. Danc· 
ing. Heraldry. (Priest, double 
slots unless Paladin) Musical In· 
slrument. 
Readlng/Wrlling. 
(Warrior) BlInd·Fighting. En· 
durance. 
Equipment: The Cavalier 
must start play with (i.e .. spend 
his initial gold on) at least twO 
weapons, Including one lance 
and one sword. and must then 
buy the most expensive SCt of 
annor he can stili afford . After 
those expenditures. whalever 
remains of his gold can be spent 
on Ilems of his choice. 
Special Benefits: The Cavalier enjoys many special benefits. including: 
At 1st level. he gets a + 1 to hit 
with any lance for which he has 
proficiency. when using the 
lance from horseback. This goes 
up + 1 every s ix experience 
levels (so he'll be +2 at 7th level. 
+ 3 at 13th. etc.). 
At 3rd level. he gets a + 1 to 
hit with anyone rypc of sword 
(his choice from among those he 
has proficiency with: most common are broad sword. long 
sword. bastard sword, and scim· 
ltar). This goes up + 1 every six 
experience levels (SO he' II be +2 
at 9th level. +3 at 15m. etc.). 
At5thlevel.hegets a + I to hit 
with either horseman's mace. 
horseman's nail. or horseman's 
pick (his choice from among 
those he has proficiency with). 
This goes up + I every six experience levels (so he' II be + 2 at 
11th level. +3 at 17th level. 
etc.). 
These pluses to hit do not add 
to damage. and don't allow the 
Cavalier to hit a monster that can 
only be hit by magical weapons. 
The Cavalier ls compietely immune to the fcar spell. Because 
he is so brave. he Inspires others 
to courage. and so. while he is 
fighting. he actuall)' radiates an 
emoCion spell In a 10' radius. 
This cmoHon spell radiates 
courage (see the writeup for the 
4th-level wizard spell emotion). 
but only to the extent that II ne· 
gates fear: it does nOI bestow the 
berserk fury that the actual wizard spell provides. 
The Cavalier is +4 to save vs. 
all magiC which would affect his 
mind. such as the wizard spells 
chann person. friends. hypno· 
tism, sleep. irrllatlon. ray or enfeeblement, scare. and geas. 
and the clerical spells command, charm persOn or mammal. enchralJ. cloak or bravery. 
and symbol. 
The Cavalier starts play with 
a horse which he docs not have 
to pay for. This will be either a 
Heavy Warhorse. Medium War· 
horse. or Light Warhorse (see 
the Monstrous Compendium. 
Volume One entry on Horses). 
The player may choose what 
sort of horse it is. subject to the 
OM's approval It will automatically be a Charger (see the section on Horse Quality In the 
Dungeon Master's Guide. page 
36): the OM may roll for Its personality traiLS according to 
those rules. If this horse dies. 
the Cavalier has to acquire himself another one through the 
usual campaign means (buy 
one. be given one for noble 
deeds. elc.). but will not be con· 
teOl wim any horse which is not 
a Warhorse of Charger quality. 
The Cavalier receives a +3 reacllon from anyone of his own 
culture (except criminals and 
characters of evil alignment. 
from whom he receives a -3). 
And finally, the Cavalier has 
the right to demand shelter. 
H 
When he travels. he can demand shelter from anyone in his 
own nalion who is of status 
lower than nobility. And most 
people of his own status or 
highcr will be happy to ofTer him 
shelter when he Is travelling. 
Special Hindrances: For all 
these benefits, the Cavalier has 
some preHy hefty hindrances as 
well. 
The Cavalier cannot attack an 
opponent at range If he can inslead charge ahead and atiack 
him in melee or Jousting com· 
bat. Therefore, he cannot snipe 
on enemies with a bow or crossbow: he cannot use a polearm 
from behind a shield wall. He 
has to be on the front Line. meet· 
Ing his foes face- to-face. (A Cav· 
alter could conceivably shoot an 
opponcnt with an arrow to stop 
that opponent from kiliingan Innocent person: that doesn't con· 
slitute a violation of his code. 
But he couldn't shoot Ule enemy to protect a friend if his 
friend is fighting that enemy 
honorably ... even if his friend 
Is losing.) 
In any combat. the Cavalier 
must attack the enemy who is 
lhe biggest and most powerfullooking. If he's held up by lesser 
troops. he must dispatch them 
as quickly as possible and then 
get to his "'real" opponent. 
He must always have the 
highest-quality armor he can afford. As he goes through his 
early experience levels. if he has 
the money. he' II constantly be 
selling his old armor and buying 
lhe next most protecllve set of 
armor. His goaJ Is to have a Sel of 
full plate armor: the next step 
down from thai is field plate. 
then plate mall. then bronze 
plate mall. then banded or 
splint, then chain. then scale or 
brlgandinc. then ring or studded. And to him. magiC bonuses 
don't mean as much as the type 
of annor: He prefers a suit of ordinary field plate to a set of
